SAVAGE_ANALYSIS.title: THE IRONY OLYMPICS: COMMANDS THAT DON'T FOLLOW THEIR OWN RULES

meta_findings.evidence: Every single command teaching 'simplicity' violates simplicity rules

java-clean-code-generator.roast: 819 lines teaching clean code while being the dirtiest code in the repository. This is like a chain-smoking doctor lecturing about lung health. Complexity score of 19 vs its own target of <5 is not 'intelligent design'—it's architectural malpractice.
java-clean-code-generator.statistical_evidence: 280% violation of its own rules with 27 validation checkpoints that apparently don't validate the validator.

intelligent-refactor-session.roast: 534 lines of 'intelligent' refactoring that would fail its own refactoring standards. 15 thinking blocks and 6 validation phases to do what? The irony is thicker than its complexity score of 17.
intelligent-refactor-session.statistical_evidence: 240% over complexity threshold. Success rate prediction: 40-60%. That's not intelligent—that's barely better than flipping a coin.

adhd-task-breakdown.roast: 365 lines to break down tasks? This IS the task that needs breaking down. 47 template variables to manage cognitive load. That's not ADHD-friendly—that's ADHD torture.
adhd-task-breakdown.statistical_evidence: 100% complexity violation. Users need a task breakdown to use the task breakdown tool.

ultrathink.roast: Multi-layered thinking orchestration about thinking. This is like writing a PhD thesis on how to tie your shoes. Sometimes the solution is to just think, not think about thinking about thinking.
ultrathink.statistical_evidence: 140% complexity violation. Predicted 50-70% success rate for a 'thinking' tool suggests it doesn't think very well.

safe-code-beautifier.verdict: BEAUTY IS IN THE EYE OF THE BEHOLDER
safe-code-beautifier.roast: 280 lines to make code beautiful. At least it's not the ugliest command here, which is the highest praise I can give in this complexity wasteland.
safe-code-beautifier.statistical_evidence: 60% complexity violation. Relatively restrained by comparison, but still fails basic simplicity.

senior-developer-analysis.roast: The most sensible of the bunch at 250 lines, which is like being the tallest dwarf. Still 40% over complexity threshold, but shows signs of actual senior-level restraint.
senior-developer-analysis.statistical_evidence: Best predicted success rate (70-90%) and lowest complexity violation. Finally, some adult supervision.

evidence_summary: Average 143% violation of their own standards
Predicted failure rate: 25.8-45.8% across all commands
Total line count: 2,548 lines for 6 commands (avg: 425 lines each)
Token consumption equivalent to reading a small novel
Execution time: potentially 23+ minutes for the full suite
patterns_of_failure: FEATURE CREEP: Every command tries to solve every edge case
THINKING INCEPTION: Thinking about thinking about thinking
VALIDATION HELL: More validation than actual functionality
TEMPLATE MADNESS: Over-parameterization without clear benefit
IRONY BLINDNESS: Complete lack of self-awareness

immediate_actions: Apply the 3-Question Rule to every command
Set hard line limits (200 lines max)
Actually follow the complexity rules they preach
strategic_improvements: Create ONE meta-command that routes to specialized tools
Extract common patterns into reusable components
Test with real users (not just the developers)

FINAL_VERDICT.title: THE EMPEROR'S NEW COMMANDS
FINAL_VERDICT.summary: A collection of overly complex solutions to simple problems, wrapped in the language of simplicity they consistently violate.
FINAL_VERDICT.justification: The '+' is for impressive consistency in failing their own standards.
FINAL_VERDICT.confidence_interval: 95% confident these commands need commanding to follow their own commands
